title: Request for Extended Library Hours
description: Formal request email for a study-related service.
prompt:
You are preparing for an important English examination, but your local library closes too early in the evening. Write an email to the library manager to:
- explain why you need longer opening hours
- describe how extended hours would help students
- suggest a practical schedule for exam season
required_points: explain the reason for the request
sentence_starters: I am writing to ask whether...
This change would help students because...
selection_reason: Task 1 request letters are common VSTEP communicative tasks and train formal purpose, reason and proposal language.

title: Complaint about an Online Course
description: Formal complaint email about service quality.
prompt:
You recently paid for an online English course, but several live lessons were cancelled and the learning platform often stopped working. Write an email to the course provider to:
- describe the problems you experienced
- explain how they affected your study plan
- request a solution or compensation
required_points: describe cancelled lessons and technical problems
explain the effect on study plans
request a clear solution or compensation
sentence_starters: I am writing to complain about...
As a result, I was unable to...
selection_reason: Complaint emails test tone control, sequencing of details and functional vocabulary for service problems.

title: Apology for Missing a Meeting
description: Semi-formal apology email with explanation and repair offer.
prompt:
You promised to attend a meeting with your project group but you could not come. Write an email to your group members to:
- apologize for missing the meeting
- explain what happened
- offer to complete a specific task for the group
required_points: offer a specific way to help
sentence_starters: To make up for this, I can...
selection_reason: Apology letters are frequent Task 1 practice types and help learners cover all required bullet points.

title: Application for a Part-time Tutor Role
description: Formal application letter for work and education context.
prompt:
You have seen an advertisement for a part-time English tutor at a community center. Write a letter to the coordinator to:
- express your interest in the position
- describe your relevant experience or skills
- ask about the schedule and payment
sentence_starters: I would like to apply for...
I believe I am suitable because...
Could you please let me know...
selection_reason: Application letters combine formal register with work-related vocabulary and clear information requests.

title: Invitation to a Cultural Festival
description: Informal invitation email for social and culture topic.
prompt:
Your city is holding a cultural festival next weekend. Write an email to an English-speaking friend to:
- invite them to join you
- describe two activities at the festival
- suggest when and where to meet
selection_reason: Invitation emails train friendly tone, arrangements and descriptive details in a familiar VSTEP topic.

title: Advice on a Healthier Routine
description: Friendly advice email on health and lifestyle.
prompt:
Your friend says they feel tired because they study late every night and do little exercise. Write an email to your friend to:
- show sympathy for their problem
- give two practical pieces of advice
- encourage them to keep a healthier routine
selection_reason: Advice letters support health-topic vocabulary and modal language such as should, could and might.

title: Asking about a Homestay Program
description: Formal information request for travel and study context.
prompt:
You want to join a short English course abroad and stay with a host family. Write an email to the program officer to:
- explain your study plan
- ask about the host family and location
- request information about costs and application deadlines
required_points: ask about host family and location
sentence_starters: I would like to know whether...
Could you also provide details about...
selection_reason: Information-request emails test clear question formation and formal register in education/travel contexts.

title: Thank-you Email after a Volunteer Event
description: Semi-formal thank-you email with feedback.
prompt:
You took part in a weekend volunteer event organized by a local club. Write an email to the organizer to:
- thank them for organizing the event
- describe what you learned from the experience
- suggest one improvement for the next event
sentence_starters: For the next event, it might be useful to...
selection_reason: Thank-you emails encourage positive tone while still requiring concrete detail and a useful suggestion.

title: Arranging Airport Pickup
description: Informal arrangement email with practical details.
prompt:
Your cousin from another country will visit your city for the first time. Write an email to your cousin to:
- confirm when you will pick them up at the airport
- explain how they can recognize you
- suggest one activity for their first evening
selection_reason: Arrangement letters train time/place details, future forms and practical travel vocabulary.

title: Feedback on Company Training
description: Formal feedback email after workplace training.
prompt:
Your company recently organized a communication skills workshop. Write an email to the human resources manager to:
- thank the company for the training
- explain which part was most useful
- suggest a topic for future training
sentence_starters: For future training, I would suggest...
selection_reason: Workplace feedback emails are realistic Task 1 prompts and support B2 functional vocabulary.

title: Online Learning after the Pandemic
description: Opinion essay on education and technology.
prompt: Some people believe that online learning should become a permanent part of university education. Others think face-to-face classes are still more effective. To what extent do you agree or disagree? Give reasons and examples.
required_points: support ideas with reasons and examples
sentence_starters: However, this does not mean that...
selection_reason: Online education is a high-frequency VSTEP/CEFR topic that elicits opinion language and balanced reasoning.

title: Improving Public Transport
description: Problem-solution essay on urban transport.
prompt: Many large cities suffer from traffic congestion and air pollution. What problems are caused by this situation, and what measures could governments take to improve public transport?
selection_reason: Urban transport appears across reading/writing topics and trains cause-effect plus solution development.

title: Social Media and Young People
description: Discussion essay on media communication.
prompt: Some people say social media helps young people communicate and learn. Others argue that it damages their mental health and concentration. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
required_points: discuss risks for mental health and concentration
sentence_starters: Supporters of social media argue that...
On the other hand, critics point out that...
selection_reason: Social media is a common contemporary topic that supports balanced discussion and evaluative vocabulary.

title: An Aging Population
description: Advantages/disadvantages essay on demographics and health.
prompt: In many countries, people are living longer than in the past. What are the advantages and disadvantages of an aging population for society? Give reasons and examples.
required_points: explain advantages of longer life expectancy
selection_reason: Aging population tasks develop health, society and policy vocabulary at B2 level.

title: Plastic Waste in Daily Life
description: Cause-effect essay on environment.
prompt: Plastic waste has become a serious problem in many communities. What are the main causes of this problem, and what effects does it have on the environment?
required_points: explain main causes of plastic waste
sentence_starters: One reason for this problem is...
selection_reason: Environment cause-effect prompts are accessible for B1 learners while still supporting VSTEP essay structure.

title: Artificial Intelligence in the Workplace
description: Opinion essay on technology and employment.
prompt: Artificial intelligence is increasingly used to make decisions in the workplace. Some people welcome this trend, while others are concerned about fairness and job security. To what extent do you think AI should be used in employment decisions?
sentence_starters: There is a strong case for...
selection_reason: AI and work supports C1 lexical range, hedging and policy argumentation without relying on copied exam text.

title: Young People Moving to Cities
description: Discussion essay on urbanization and opportunity.
prompt: Many young people move from rural areas to big cities for education and work. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this trend for both young people and their hometowns.
sentence_starters: For young people, city life can...
selection_reason: Urbanization is already present in VSTEP-style samples and offers strong argument coverage for B2 learners.

title: Unhealthy Lifestyles among Students
description: Problem-solution essay on student health.
prompt: Many students do not get enough sleep or exercise because they spend too much time studying or using digital devices. What problems can this cause, and what solutions would you suggest?
selection_reason: Health/lifestyle prompts are familiar enough for B1 but require clear cause and solution organization.

title: Tourism and Local Communities
description: Advantages/disadvantages essay on tourism.
prompt: Tourism brings income to many local communities, but it can also create environmental and cultural problems. What are the advantages and disadvantages of tourism for local people?
sentence_starters: Tourism can benefit local people because...
selection_reason: Tourism is a high-utility VSTEP domain combining economy, culture and environment vocabulary.

title: Consumerism and Environmental Responsibility
description: C1 essay on consumption and sustainability.
prompt: Modern economies often depend on people buying more goods and services. However, this pattern may harm the environment. Should governments limit consumerism, or should individuals be free to choose how much they buy?
selection_reason: This C1 prompt encourages abstract argumentation, concession and formal vocabulary around policy trade-offs.
